key_points: Always explain WHY you want the job (motivation)
Use formal adjectives: teamfähig, zuverlässig, engagiert
zur Verfügung stehen = available (formal)
Close with KII: Ich würde mich freuen...

videos.title: B1 - Lesson 37 | Jobsuche Teil 3 | Interview Phrases | Learn German Intermediate

study_notes.summary: Part 3 covers Vorstellungsgespräch (job interview) phrases: how to introduce yourself professionally, answer typical questions (Erzählen Sie über sich, Warum möchten Sie bei uns arbeiten?, Was sind Ihre Stärken/Schwächen?), ask questions, and negotiate. Directly useful for TELC B1 Sprechen Teil 1 (self-intro) and professional vocabulary.
study_notes.key_concept: Interview structure: 1) Greeting + self-introduction, 2) Career summary ('Ich habe ... studiert und arbeite seit ... Jahren als...'), 3) Motivation ('Was mich an dieser Stelle reizt, ist...'), 4) Strengths + example, 5) Your questions ('Dürfte ich fragen, ob/wie/wann...'). Use formal register throughout.
study_notes.grammar_rule: Self-intro: 'Mein Name ist... Ich bin ... von Beruf / Ich arbeite als...' Career: 'Ich habe ... studiert / eine Ausbildung gemacht.' Duration: 'seit + Dativ': 'Ich arbeite seit drei Jahren in diesem Bereich.' Questions: 'Dürfte ich fragen, ob + Nebensatz' / 'Könnten Sie mir sagen, wann + Nebensatz'
study_notes.word_order_note: 'seit' takes Dativ and Präsens tense (not Perfekt!): 'Ich arbeite seit 2020 hier.' (not 'habe gearbeitet'). Indirect questions: 'Könnten Sie mir sagen, wann der Vertrag beginnt?' (verb at end in Nebensatz).
common_mistakes: Using Perfekt with seit: 'Ich habe seit 3 Jahren gearbeitet' → 'Ich arbeite seit 3 Jahren' (Präsens!)
Being too casual: 'Ich bin Peter, ich will den Job' → 'Mein Name ist Peter Schmidt. Ich interessiere mich für diese Position.'
Listing only weaknesses without positive spin: 'Ich bin ungeduldig' → 'Manchmal bin ich ungeduldig, aber ich arbeite daran.'
Not asking questions at the end: always prepare 2-3 questions to show interest
Forgetting Dativ with 'bei': 'Ich möchte bei Ihrem Unternehmen arbeiten' → 'bei Ihrer Firma' (Dativ!)

vocabulary.example_de: Mein Name ist Anna. Ich bin Informatikerin und arbeite seit 5 Jahren im IT-Bereich.

flashcards.back: PRÄSENS! 'Ich arbeite seit 3 Jahren hier.' (not Perfekt!)
Tell us about yourself. — Start with: Name, Beruf, Erfahrung, Motivation.
Might I ask if...? (very polite KII question + Nebensatz)
by profession — Ich bin Ingenieur von Beruf.
vocational training — Ich habe eine Ausbildung als Koch gemacht.

bei Ihrer Firma / bei diesem Unternehmen (Dativ!)

For Sprechen Teil 1, prepare a 2-minute self-intro: Name, Herkunft, Beruf/Studium, seit wann in Deutschland, Hobbys.
Use indirect questions for politeness: 'Könnten Sie mir sagen, ob...' instead of 'Ist...?' — sounds much more professional.

visual_summary.title: Jobsuche Teil 3 — Interview Phrases

My name is... I am a ... by profession.
Ich arbeite seit X Jahren als...
I've been working for X years as...

key_points: seit + Dativ + PRÄSENS (never Perfekt!)
Frame weaknesses positively
Use KII for polite questions: Dürfte/Könnten
Always prepare 2-3 questions for the interviewer

videos.title: B1 - Lesson 38 | Jobsuche Teil 4 | Workplace Vocabulary | Learn German Intermediate

study_notes.summary: Part 4 covers workplace vocabulary and expressions: types of work (Vollzeit, Teilzeit, Minijob), workplace culture (Arbeitsklima, Kollegen), work conditions (Überstunden, Urlaub, Vertrag), and talking about your daily work routine. Integrates grammar like Nebensätze and Konnektoren to describe work situations.
study_notes.key_concept: Work vocabulary categories: Employment types (Vollzeit/Teilzeit/befristet/unbefristet), Documents (Arbeitsvertrag/Kündigung/Zeugnis), Daily work (Besprechung/Termin/Projekt/Deadline), Benefits (Urlaubstage/Fortbildung/Betriebsrente). Use 'Ich bin zuständig für + Akk.' to describe responsibilities.
study_notes.grammar_rule: Responsibility: 'Ich bin zuständig für + Akk.' (I am responsible for). Describing tasks: 'Zu meinen Aufgaben gehört es, ... zu + Inf.' (Among my tasks is to...). Work conditions: 'Mein Vertrag ist befristet/unbefristet.' Opinions about work: 'Was mir an meiner Arbeit gefällt, ist...'
study_notes.word_order_note: 'Zu meinen Aufgaben gehört es, ...' is followed by zu-Infinitiv: 'Zu meinen Aufgaben gehört es, Kunden zu beraten.' 'Was mir gefällt, ist...' starts with a was-clause (verb at end): 'Was mir an der Arbeit gefällt, ist die Abwechslung.'
common_mistakes: Confusing befristet (temporary) and unbefristet (permanent) — critical for contracts!
Saying 'Arbeitzeit' instead of 'Arbeitszeit' — compound nouns with -s-: Arbeit-s-zeit
Using 'Job' too informally: prefer 'Stelle', 'Arbeit', 'Position' in formal contexts
Forgetting gender: der Vertrag, die Kündigung, das Gehalt, die Besprechung
Not knowing work-related compound nouns: Weiterbildung, Arbeitsklima, Betriebsrat

In Hören, workplace conversations appear frequently. Listen for: Vertrag, Kündigung, Überstunden, Besprechung, Termin.
For Sprechen Teil 1, describe your work: 'Ich arbeite als... bei... Ich bin zuständig für... Was mir besonders gefällt, ist...'

visual_summary.title: Jobsuche Teil 4 — Workplace Vocabulary
key_points: befristet (temporary) vs. unbefristet (permanent) — know the difference!
zuständig für + Akk. for describing responsibilities
Compound nouns: break them down to understand (Arbeits-klima, Arbeits-vertrag)
Common in Lesen Teil 3 job ad matching exercises

videos.title: B1 - Lesson 39 | Futur I | Future Tense | werden + Infinitiv | Learn German Intermediate

study_notes.summary: Futur I (Future I) in German uses 'werden + Infinitiv' to express future plans, predictions, and assumptions. Formation: 'Ich werde morgen kommen.' While German often uses Präsens + time expression for the future ('Ich komme morgen'), Futur I adds emphasis on intention, prediction, or assumption.
study_notes.key_concept: Futur I = werden + Infinitiv. 'werden' is conjugated, Infinitiv goes to the END. Three main uses: 1) Future plans/intentions: 'Ich werde nächstes Jahr studieren.' 2) Predictions: 'Es wird morgen regnen.' 3) Assumptions: 'Er wird wohl schon schlafen.' (He's probably already sleeping.) For near future, Germans usually prefer Präsens + time: 'Ich gehe morgen einkaufen.'
study_notes.grammar_rule: Conjugation of werden: ich werde, du wirst, er wird, wir werden, ihr werdet, sie werden. Structure: S + werden(conj.) + ... + Infinitiv(end). In Nebensatz: '...weil ich morgen arbeiten werde.' (werden at end after Infinitiv). Assumption marker: 'wohl/wahrscheinlich' + Futur I.
study_notes.word_order_note: Main clause: 'Ich werde nächstes Jahr nach Berlin ziehen.' (werden pos.2, Inf. at end). Nebensatz: '...weil er nächste Woche kommen wird.' (Infinitiv before wird at end). With modal: very rare/avoided — use Präsens instead.
common_mistakes: Confusing werden (future auxiliary) with werden (become) and werden (passive auxiliary)
Overusing Futur I when Präsens + time word is more natural: 'Morgen gehe ich einkaufen' (not 'Morgen werde ich einkaufen gehen')
Wrong word order: Infinitiv must go to END: 'Ich werde morgen kommen' (not 'Ich werde kommen morgen')
Wrong werden conjugation: du wirst (not werdest), er wird (not werdet)
In Nebensatz, placing werden before Infinitiv: '...dass ich kommen werde' (not '...dass ich werde kommen')

vocabulary.example_de: Ich werde nächstes Jahr heiraten.
vocabulary.example_en: I will get married next year.

vocabulary.example_de: Es wird wahrscheinlich regnen.
vocabulary.example_en: It will probably rain.

vocabulary.example_de: Sie wird wohl schon zu Hause sein.
vocabulary.example_en: She is probably already at home.

vocabulary.example_de: Das wird bestimmt toll!
vocabulary.example_en: That will certainly be great!

vocabulary.example_de: Irgendwann werde ich nach Japan reisen.
vocabulary.example_en: Someday I will travel to Japan.

vocabulary.example_de: In Zukunft werde ich mehr Sport treiben.
vocabulary.example_en: In the future I will exercise more.

vocabulary.example_de: Er wird bald ankommen.
vocabulary.example_en: He will arrive soon.

vocabulary.example_de: Ich verspreche, ich werde pünktlich sein.
vocabulary.example_en: I promise, I will be on time.

grammar_patterns.example_de: Wir werden nächstes Jahr umziehen.
grammar_patterns.example_en: We will move next year.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Future tense structure — tested in Sprachbausteine

grammar_patterns.example_de: Er wird wohl krank sein.
grammar_patterns.example_en: He is probably sick. (assumption)
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Understanding assumptions in Lesen and Hören passages

grammar_patterns.example_de: Ich gehe morgen zum Arzt. (= Ich werde morgen zum Arzt gehen.)
grammar_patterns.example_en: I'm going to the doctor tomorrow.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Both are correct — Präsens is more common in spoken German

grammar_patterns.example_de: Ich glaube, dass er morgen kommen wird.
grammar_patterns.example_en: I believe that he will come tomorrow.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Nebensatz word order with werden — Infinitiv BEFORE werden at end

flashcards.back: werden + Infinitiv: Ich werde kommen. Er wird arbeiten. Wir werden lernen.
ich werde, du wirst, er/sie wird, wir werden, ihr werdet, sie/Sie werden
1) Future plan: 'Ich werde studieren.' 2) Prediction: 'Es wird regnen.' 3) Assumption: 'Er wird wohl schlafen.'
Infinitiv BEFORE werden: '...dass er kommen wird.' (not ...dass er wird kommen)
Both work for future. Präsens + time word is more natural in speech. Futur I emphasizes intention/prediction.
Assumption: 'Sie wird wohl schon schlafen.' (She is probably already sleeping.)

flashcards.front: Er wird wahrscheinlich zu spät kommen.
flashcards.back: He will probably come late. (Futur I as prediction)
Someday I will... — expressing future wishes/plans

practice_sentences.de: Ich __________ nächstes Jahr nach Spanien __________.
practice_sentences.en: I will travel to Spain next year.

practice_sentences.de: Es __________ morgen wahrscheinlich __________.
practice_sentences.en: It will probably rain tomorrow.

practice_sentences.de: Sie __________ __________ schon angekommen sein.
practice_sentences.en: She has probably already arrived. (assumption)

practice_sentences.de: Ich glaube, dass er bald __________ __________.
practice_sentences.en: I believe that he will come soon.
practice_sentences.hint: kommen wird (Nebensatz: Inf. before werden at end)

practice_sentences.de: In Zukunft __________ ich weniger Zucker __________.
practice_sentences.en: In the future I will eat less sugar.
practice_sentences.hint: werde ... essen (future intention with Futur I)

exam_tips.tip: In Sprachbausteine, you may need to choose 'werden' to complete a Futur I sentence. Know: ich werde, du wirst, er wird.
In Hören, Futur I with 'wohl/wahrscheinlich' signals an ASSUMPTION — the speaker is guessing, not stating a fact.
For Schreiben, use Futur I for promises and plans: 'Ich werde pünktlich sein.' / 'Wir werden das Problem lösen.'
Don't confuse 3 uses of werden: 1) Future: 'Ich werde kommen' 2) Passive: 'Es wird gemacht' 3) Become: 'Er wird Arzt.'

visual_summary.title: Futur I — werden + Infinitiv
key_points: werden + Infinitiv = Futur I
3 uses: plan, prediction, assumption (wohl)
Präsens + time is more common for near future
Nebensatz: Infinitiv before werden at end

videos.title: Lesen · Teil 1 — Globalverstehen (Headlines to Texts)

study_notes.summary: Lesen Teil 1 tests your ability to match newspaper headlines with short text summaries. You have 5 headlines and 10 short texts — you must find the correct match for each. This is a global reading exercise: you don't need every word, just the main idea.
study_notes.key_concept: Globalverstehen — grasping the main idea without reading every detail. Focus on keywords (Schlüsselwörter) in both the headline and the text.
study_notes.grammar_rule: Headlines often use Nominalisierung (noun-heavy style): 'Steigende Mieten in Berlin' instead of 'Die Mieten in Berlin steigen.' Learn to convert between these forms.
study_notes.word_order_note: German headlines frequently drop articles and verbs. 'Neues Gesetz gegen Plastikmüll' = 'Ein neues Gesetz gegen Plastikmüll wurde verabschiedet.'
common_mistakes: Reading every word instead of scanning for keywords
Matching based on a single shared word rather than overall meaning
Ignoring distractors — two texts may share a topic but have different main ideas
Spending too much time on one pair instead of moving forward

vocabulary.example_de: Lesen Sie die Überschriften und ordnen Sie zu.
vocabulary.example_en: Read the headlines and match them.

vocabulary.example_de: Ordnen Sie jede Überschrift einem Text zu.
vocabulary.example_en: Match each headline to a text.

vocabulary.example_de: Lesen Sie jeden Abschnitt sorgfältig.
vocabulary.example_en: Read each section carefully.

vocabulary.example_de: Unterstreichen Sie die Schlüsselwörter.
vocabulary.example_en: Underline the keywords.

vocabulary.example_de: Was ist die Hauptidee des Textes?
vocabulary.example_en: What is the main idea of the text?

vocabulary.example_de: Überfliegen Sie den Text zuerst.
vocabulary.example_en: Skim the text first.

vocabulary.example_de: Der Zusammenhang zwischen Titel und Text ist wichtig.
vocabulary.example_en: The connection between title and text is important.

vocabulary.example_de: Schreiben Sie eine kurze Zusammenfassung.
vocabulary.example_en: Write a short summary.

grammar_patterns.structure: Verb → Noun: steigen → die Steigerung, wachsen → das Wachstum
grammar_patterns.example_de: Die Steigerung der Preise betrifft alle.
grammar_patterns.example_en: The increase in prices affects everyone.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Headlines in Teil 1 frequently use nominalized forms

grammar_patterns.example_de: Ein neues Gesetz wurde beschlossen.
grammar_patterns.example_en: A new law was passed.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Understanding shortened passive in headlines is essential

grammar_patterns.example_de: Neue Umweltschutzmaßnahmen geplant.
grammar_patterns.example_en: New environmental protection measures planned.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Breaking down compound nouns helps decode headline meaning

flashcards.back: Understanding the general/main idea of a text without reading every word

flashcards.front: How many headlines in Lesen Teil 1?
flashcards.back: 5 headlines to match with 10 texts (5 are distractors)
to skim/scan a text quickly
Match headline to text by finding shared keywords and main idea
Die Mieten steigen (Rising rents = Rents are rising)
About 10 minutes — scan quickly, don't read word by word
A keyword — the most important word in a sentence that carries the core meaning
Two texts share a topic word but have different main ideas — always check the FULL meaning

practice_sentences.de: Lesen Sie zuerst alle Überschriften durch.
practice_sentences.en: First read through all the headlines.
practice_sentences.hint: Start with headlines to know what topics to expect

practice_sentences.de: Unterstreichen Sie die wichtigsten Wörter in jedem Text.
practice_sentences.en: Underline the most important words in each text.

practice_sentences.de: Wenn zwei Texte ähnlich sind, achten Sie auf die Details.
practice_sentences.en: If two texts are similar, pay attention to the details.

practice_sentences.de: Die Überschrift fasst die Hauptidee zusammen.
practice_sentences.en: The headline summarizes the main idea.

practice_sentences.de: Arbeiten Sie systematisch: ein Text nach dem anderen.
practice_sentences.en: Work systematically: one text after another.

exam_tips.tip: Read ALL 5 headlines first before looking at any texts. This primes your brain for what to look for.
For each text, ask: 'What is the ONE main point?' Then find the headline that matches that point.
Beware of word-level traps: 'Schule' in both a headline and text doesn't mean they match — check the full meaning.
Cross out headlines as you match them to narrow down remaining options.

visual_summary.title: Lesen Teil 1 — Strategy Overview

rows: Match to headline, cross it off

key_points: Globalverstehen = main idea, not details
5 headlines + 10 texts = 5 distractors
Keywords in headline ↔ keywords in text
Don't be fooled by surface-level word matches

videos.title: Lesen · Teil 2 — Detailverstehen (Multiple Choice)

study_notes.summary: Lesen Teil 2 is a detail reading comprehension exercise. You read a longer text (newspaper article, report, or essay) and answer 5 multiple-choice questions with 3 options each (a, b, c). You need to understand specific details, not just the main idea.
study_notes.key_concept: Detailverstehen — understanding specific information, opinions, and arguments in a text. The correct answer is often a paraphrase (Umformulierung) of what the text says.
study_notes.grammar_rule: Watch for negation and qualification: 'nicht alle' ≠ 'keine', 'manchmal' ≠ 'immer'. TELC loves testing whether you catch these subtle differences.
study_notes.word_order_note: Questions follow the order of the text — Question 1 relates to the beginning, Question 5 to the end. Use this to locate answers efficiently.
common_mistakes: Choosing an answer because it uses the same words as the text (often a distractor)
Not reading all three options before deciding
Missing negation words: nicht, kein, nie, selten, kaum
Bringing in outside knowledge instead of answering from the text only

vocabulary.example_de: Lesen Sie den Bericht über Umweltschutz.
vocabulary.example_en: Read the report about environmental protection.

vocabulary.example_de: Welche Aussage ist richtig?
vocabulary.example_en: Which statement is correct?

vocabulary.example_de: Laut dem Text steigen die Preise.
vocabulary.example_en: According to the text, prices are rising.

vocabulary.example_de: Die richtige Antwort ist oft eine Umformulierung.
vocabulary.example_en: The correct answer is often a paraphrase.

vocabulary.example_de: Vorsicht vor Ablenkern bei Multiple-Choice.
vocabulary.example_en: Watch out for distractors in multiple choice.

vocabulary.example_de: Was ist die Meinung des Autors?

vocabulary.example_en: The author justifies his opinion.

vocabulary.example_de: Im Gegensatz zu früher arbeiten mehr Frauen.
vocabulary.example_en: In contrast to before, more women work.

grammar_patterns.structure: Er sagt, er habe / sie sei / man könne
grammar_patterns.example_de: Der Experte sagt, die Situation sei ernst.
grammar_patterns.example_en: The expert says the situation is serious.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Newspaper articles use Konjunktiv I for reported speech — recognize it to identify opinions vs facts

grammar_patterns.structure: fast, kaum, selten, manchmal, häufig, meistens
grammar_patterns.example_de: Fast alle Teilnehmer bestanden die Prüfung.
grammar_patterns.example_en: Almost all participants passed the exam.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Distractors often change 'fast alle' to 'alle' — tiny word, big meaning change

grammar_patterns.structure: weil, da, deshalb, daher, aus diesem Grund, aufgrund
grammar_patterns.example_de: Aufgrund des schlechten Wetters fiel das Fest aus.
grammar_patterns.example_en: Due to the bad weather, the festival was canceled.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Questions often ask WHY something happened — find the cause connector

flashcards.front: How many questions in Lesen Teil 2?
flashcards.back: 5 multiple-choice questions with 3 options each (a, b, c)
Understanding specific details and information in a text, not just the general idea
A paraphrase — saying the same thing with different words. Correct TELC answers are usually paraphrases.
These mean different things! 'fast alle' = almost all, 'alle' = all. TELC tests this distinction.
according to the text — answer based on what the TEXT says, not your own knowledge
Yes! Q1 = beginning of text, Q5 = end. Use this to locate answers.
distractor — a wrong answer designed to look correct (often uses same words as text)
Read the question + all options FIRST, then find the relevant paragraph in the text

practice_sentences.de: Lesen Sie die Fragen vor dem Text.
practice_sentences.en: Read the questions before the text.
practice_sentences.hint: This helps you know what to look for

practice_sentences.de: Die richtige Antwort verwendet oft andere Wörter als der Text.
practice_sentences.en: The correct answer often uses different words than the text.

practice_sentences.de: Achten Sie auf kleine Wörter wie 'nicht', 'kaum', 'nur'.
practice_sentences.en: Pay attention to small words like 'not', 'hardly', 'only'.

practice_sentences.de: Laut dem Autor ist Bildung der wichtigste Faktor.

practice_sentences.de: Streichen Sie die falsche Antwort zuerst durch.
practice_sentences.en: Cross out the wrong answer first.

exam_tips.tip: Read ALL three options before choosing — the third option might be a better fit than the first one you liked.
If two options seem correct, the one that paraphrases the text (rather than copying exact words) is usually right.
When the question asks about someone's opinion, look for reporting verbs: sagt, meint, findet, glaubt, ist der Meinung.
Negation trap: if the text says 'Er hat nicht gesagt, dass...' don't choose an option that states he said it.

visual_summary.title: Lesen Teil 2 — MCQ Strategy

rows: Cross out clearly wrong ones first

key_points: Correct answer = paraphrase of text
Distractor = looks right but changes a detail
Small words change everything (nicht, kaum, nur)
Answer from the TEXT, not from your knowledge

videos.title: Lesen · Teil 3 — Selektives Lesen (People → Notices)

study_notes.summary: Lesen Teil 3 is selective reading: 10 people each need something specific, and you must match them to the correct advertisement or notice from a list of 12 options (2 are distractors). This tests your ability to scan for specific information quickly.
study_notes.key_concept: Selektives Lesen — scanning for specific details rather than reading everything. Each person has 2-3 requirements. The correct ad must match ALL requirements, not just one.
study_notes.grammar_rule: Conditional and requirement expressions: sucht...mit, braucht...die/das, möchte gerne...wo, interessiert sich für...die
study_notes.word_order_note: Person descriptions use modals: 'Sie möchte einen Kurs besuchen, der am Wochenende stattfindet.' The subordinate clause (der am Wochenende stattfindet) contains the KEY requirement.
common_mistakes: Matching based on only ONE requirement instead of ALL listed requirements
Confusing similar ads (two language courses, but different times/levels)
Missing age restrictions or location requirements in ads
Not reading the full person description — the last sentence often has the critical detail

An ad matches ONE requirement but NOT all. You must check ALL requirements match.
advertisement/notice — the items you match people to

1) Underline each person's requirements, 2) Scan ads for ALL matches, 3) Cross off used ads
to be suitable for — 'Welche Anzeige eignet sich für Person 1?'
Spend ~1 min per person-ad match. Do the easy/obvious ones first.

Check for hidden requirements at the END of each person description — TELC often puts the decisive detail there.
Each ad can only be used ONCE. If you assign one, cross it off your list immediately.

visual_summary.title: Lesen Teil 3 — Matching Strategy
key_points: 10 people + 12 ads = 2 distractors
ALL requirements must match, not just one

videos.title: Lesen — Overall Strategy & Time Management

study_notes.summary: The Lesen section is worth 75 points (25% of total). You have about 65 minutes for all three parts. Time management is critical — many students fail because they spend too long on Teil 2 and rush Teil 3.
study_notes.key_concept: Budget your time: ~10 min Tel 1, ~25 min Teil 2, ~15 min Teil 3, ~15 min buffer/review. Teil 2 is the hardest but Teil 3 has the most points per minute if you're efficient.
study_notes.grammar_rule: Cross-part skills: Nominalisierung (Teil 1), Paraphrase recognition (Teil 2), Relative clauses for requirements (Teil 3). These three grammar areas cover 80% of what you need.
study_notes.word_order_note: In exam conditions, always read the QUESTIONS or TASKS first, then the text. This is true for all three Teile.
common_mistakes: Spending all time on Teil 2 and not finishing Teil 3
Reading every word when scanning is more efficient
Not transferring answers to the answer sheet (separate from the question booklet!)
Second-guessing correct answers and changing them

vocabulary.example_en: Thinking too long is a waste of time.

grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Prioritize parts where you can score most efficiently

flashcards.back: 75 points — 25% of the total TELC B1 exam (300 points)
~65 minutes total: ~10 min Teil 1, ~25 min Teil 2, ~15 min Teil 3, ~15 min buffer
60% on the written section overall (Lesen + Sprachbausteine + Hören + Schreiben combined)

Spending too long on one difficult question in Teil 2. Move on and come back later.

videos.title: Hören · Teil 1 — Globalverstehen (True/False)

study_notes.summary: Hören Teil 1 plays 5 short audio clips (announcements, messages, conversations). For each, you answer one true/false (richtig/falsch) question. You hear each clip ONCE only. Focus on the overall message, not every word.
study_notes.key_concept: Globalverstehen beim Hören — grasping the main point of a spoken text. You won't understand everything, and that's fine. Listen for the KEY information that answers the specific question.
study_notes.grammar_rule: Spoken German uses contractions and reductions: 'ne statt eine', 'hab' statt habe', 'isses statt ist es'. Train your ear for these informal forms.
study_notes.word_order_note: In spoken announcements, the most important information often comes at the END: '...und deshalb ist die Straße bis Freitag gesperrt.' The conclusion carries the answer.
common_mistakes: Panicking after missing a word and losing focus for the rest of the clip
Not reading the question BEFORE the audio plays
Confusing richtig/falsch because of negation in the question or audio
Trying to understand every word instead of listening for the key point

vocabulary.example_de: Hören Sie die Durchsage am Bahnhof.
vocabulary.example_en: Listen to the announcement at the train station.

vocabulary.example_de: Sie hören eine Nachricht auf dem Anrufbeantworter.
vocabulary.example_en: You hear a message on the answering machine.

vocabulary.example_de: Entscheiden Sie: richtig oder falsch?
vocabulary.example_en: Decide: true or false?

vocabulary.example_de: Auf dem Anrufbeantworter ist eine Nachricht.
vocabulary.example_en: There is a message on the answering machine.

vocabulary.example_de: Der Termin wurde abgesagt.
vocabulary.example_en: The appointment was canceled.

vocabulary.example_de: Das Meeting wird auf Montag verschoben.
vocabulary.example_en: The meeting is postponed to Monday.

vocabulary.example_de: Der Zug hat 20 Minuten Verspätung.
vocabulary.example_en: The train is 20 minutes late.

vocabulary.example_de: Achten Sie auf die Ansage.
vocabulary.example_en: Pay attention to the announcement.

grammar_patterns.structure: 'ne = eine, hab' = habe, is' = ist, 'nen = einen
grammar_patterns.example_de: Ich hab' 'ne Frage.
grammar_patterns.example_en: I have a question.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Audio uses natural spoken German with contractions — train your ear

grammar_patterns.structure: Statement: 'Der Laden hat zu.' Question: 'Der Laden hat geöffnet.' → FALSCH
grammar_patterns.example_de: Der Laden hat heute nicht geöffnet.
grammar_patterns.example_en: The store is not open today.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Questions often state the OPPOSITE of what you hear — listen for negation

grammar_patterns.example_de: Der Kurs wurde auf Donnerstag verschoben.
grammar_patterns.example_en: The course was postponed to Thursday.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Hören Teil 1 loves testing schedule changes

flashcards.front: How many clips in Hören Teil 1?
flashcards.back: 5 short clips, each with 1 richtig/falsch question. You hear each clip ONCE.
Understanding the MAIN POINT of what you hear, without catching every word

flashcards.front: When should you read the question?
flashcards.back: BEFORE the audio plays! Use the pause time to read and predict.
eine (spoken contraction): 'Ich hab' 'ne Frage' = 'Ich habe eine Frage'
verschieben = postpone (to a new time), absagen = cancel (not happening at all)

flashcards.front: Where is key info in announcements?
flashcards.back: Often at the END — listen all the way through, don't decide too early

flashcards.front: What if you miss a word?
flashcards.back: Don't panic! Keep listening. One missed word doesn't mean you can't answer.
Train/flight announcements, voicemail messages, store/office closures, schedule changes

practice_sentences.de: Lesen Sie die Aufgabe, bevor Sie das Audio hören.
practice_sentences.en: Read the task before you hear the audio.

practice_sentences.de: Konzentrieren Sie sich auf die Schlüsselwörter.
practice_sentences.en: Concentrate on the keywords.

practice_sentences.de: Wenn Sie etwas nicht verstehen, hören Sie weiter zu.
practice_sentences.en: If you don't understand something, keep listening.
practice_sentences.hint: Don't get stuck on one word

practice_sentences.de: Die wichtigste Information kommt oft am Ende.
practice_sentences.en: The most important information often comes at the end.

practice_sentences.de: Achten Sie auf Verneinungen wie 'nicht', 'kein', 'nie'.
practice_sentences.en: Watch out for negations like 'not', 'no', 'never'.

exam_tips.tip: Read the question in the 10-second pause BEFORE each clip. Predict what you'll hear.
For richtig/falsch: listen for whether the audio CONFIRMS or CONTRADICTS the statement. Don't overthink it.
Common trap: audio says 'verschoben auf Mittwoch' but question says 'findet am Dienstag statt' → FALSCH.
If unsure, go with your first impression. You only hear it once — no time to deliberate.

visual_summary.title: Hören Teil 1 — Quick Strategy

rows: Listen for key info, not every word
Read next question in the pause

key_points: 5 clips × 1 richtig/falsch = easy points if prepared
Read question BEFORE audio
Key info often at the END of clip
Don't panic if you miss a word

videos.title: Hören · Teil 2 — Detailverstehen (Multiple Choice)

study_notes.summary: Hören Teil 2 plays a longer conversation or interview (~3-4 minutes). You answer 5 multiple-choice questions (a, b, c) about specific details. You hear the audio TWICE, which gives you two chances to catch the answers.
study_notes.key_concept: Detailverstehen — listening for specific facts, numbers, opinions, and reasons. Unlike Teil 1, you need precision here. Use the first listen for overall understanding, the second listen to confirm specific answers.
study_notes.grammar_rule: Interview language often uses indirect speech and reported opinions: 'Sie meinte, dass...', 'Er hat gesagt, er würde...'. Distinguish between the interviewer's questions and the interviewee's actual answers.
study_notes.word_order_note: In spoken German, the verb in dass-clauses should go to the end, but speakers often use V2 order: 'Er sagt, dass er kommt morgen' instead of correct 'dass er morgen kommt'. Both mean the same.
common_mistakes: Using the first listen to answer and not actually listening the second time
Choosing an answer because you heard those exact words (distractor trap)
Missing the speaker's REAL opinion vs what they describe other people thinking
Not distinguishing between what was true BEFORE vs what is true NOW

grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: The part after 'aber' usually contains the speaker's REAL opinion

5 MCQ with 3 options (a, b, c)
Get the general picture + try to answer easy questions. Mark uncertain ones.
Focus on questions you're unsure about. Confirm your answers.

flashcards.front: 'zwar...aber' — which part is the real opinion?
flashcards.back: The part after 'aber' — 'zwar stressig, aber interessant' → overall positive
An option uses exact words from the audio but in a DIFFERENT context than what's being asked
Questions usually ask about NOW (jetzt/heute). Don't pick what was true in the past (früher).
Gespräch = general conversation (peers), Interview = one person questions another (expert)

practice_sentences.en: On the first listen: Try to understand the main idea.

practice_sentences.de: Beim zweiten Hören: Konzentrieren Sie sich auf offene Fragen.

practice_sentences.hint: Different speakers may have different opinions

practice_sentences.de: Die Antwort ist oft eine Umformulierung des Gesagten.

exam_tips.tip: Between the two plays, quickly review which questions you're uncertain about. Use the second listen strategically.
If two speakers disagree, make sure you identify WHICH speaker the question asks about.
Numbers, dates, and times are popular question targets. Write them down as you hear them.
The correct option usually paraphrases the audio. If an option uses the EXACT same phrase, it might be a trap.

visual_summary.title: Hören Teil 2 — Two-Listen Strategy

rows: Answer what you can, mark uncertainties

key_points: You hear it TWICE — use each listen differently
1st = overview, 2nd = targeted
Paraphrase = likely correct, exact words = possible trap
Track WHO says WHAT — multiple speakers!

videos.title: Hören · Teil 3 — Selektives Hören (Matching)

study_notes.summary: Hören Teil 3 tests selective listening: you hear 5 short statements or announcements, and match each to the correct description from a list. Like Lesen Teil 3 but with audio. You hear each statement ONCE.
study_notes.key_concept: Selektives Hören — listening for specific key information to make a match. You need to catch WHO needs WHAT or WHERE/WHEN something happens. Each statement has one unique matching criterion.
study_notes.grammar_rule: Statements use location/time/purpose markers: 'am Montag um 14 Uhr', 'im dritten Stock', 'für Kinder ab 6 Jahren'. These specific details are what you match against.
study_notes.word_order_note: Announcements often front important info: 'Am Samstag, den 15. März, findet...' — the date/time at the start is usually the key matching detail.
common_mistakes: Not reading the matching options before the audio plays
Getting confused between similar options (two events, different times)
Missing the one distinguishing detail (age limit, day, location)
Not marking answers immediately — forgetting by the next clip

vocabulary.example_de: Ordnen Sie jede Aussage der passenden Beschreibung zu.
vocabulary.example_en: Match each statement to the fitting description.

vocabulary.example_de: Sie hören fünf kurze Aussagen.
vocabulary.example_en: You hear five short statements.

vocabulary.example_de: Welche Beschreibung passt?
vocabulary.example_en: Which description fits?

vocabulary.example_de: Die Abteilung für Kinderbekleidung ist im 2. Stock.
vocabulary.example_en: The children's clothing department is on the 2nd floor.

vocabulary.example_de: Die Öffnungszeiten sind von 9 bis 18 Uhr.
vocabulary.example_en: Opening hours are from 9 to 6.

vocabulary.example_de: Es gibt eine Ermäßigung für Senioren.
vocabulary.example_en: There is a discount for seniors.

vocabulary.example_de: Die Veranstaltung beginnt um 19 Uhr.
vocabulary.example_en: The event starts at 7 PM.

vocabulary.example_de: Eine Anmeldung ist erforderlich.
vocabulary.example_en: Registration is required.

grammar_patterns.structure: am + day, um + time, vom...bis, ab + date
grammar_patterns.example_de: Am Freitag um 15 Uhr beginnt der Workshop.
grammar_patterns.example_en: The workshop starts on Friday at 3 PM.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Time details are the most common matching criteria in Teil 3

grammar_patterns.structure: im + Stock/Raum, in der + Abteilung, vor dem + Gebäude
grammar_patterns.example_de: Der Kurs findet im Raum 204 statt.
grammar_patterns.example_en: The course takes place in Room 204.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Location details differentiate similar-sounding options

grammar_patterns.structure: nur für + Akk, ab + age, bis zu + limit
grammar_patterns.example_de: Das Angebot gilt nur für Mitglieder.
grammar_patterns.example_en: The offer is only for members.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Restrictions narrow down matches — listen for 'nur', 'ab', 'bis'

flashcards.front: How many clips in Hören Teil 3?
flashcards.back: 5 short statements, each heard ONCE, matched to descriptions from a list
Read ALL matching options BEFORE audio starts. Underline key differences between options.
Time, place, price, age limits, who it's for — the specific details that differentiate options
'am Montag um 14 Uhr' = on Monday at 2 PM — temporal prepositions for matching

flashcards.front: What if two options seem similar?
flashcards.back: They differ by ONE detail (time, age, location). That's the detail you must catch.
matching/assignment — the core task of Teil 3
You hear each clip ONCE only. If you wait, you'll forget by the next clip.
registration required — common restriction in announcements

practice_sentences.de: Lesen Sie alle Beschreibungen, bevor das Audio beginnt.
practice_sentences.en: Read all descriptions before the audio starts.

practice_sentences.de: Achten Sie auf Uhrzeiten und Wochentage.
practice_sentences.en: Pay attention to times and days of the week.
practice_sentences.hint: These are the most common differentiators

practice_sentences.de: Markieren Sie Ihre Antwort sofort nach jeder Aussage.
practice_sentences.en: Mark your answer immediately after each statement.

practice_sentences.de: Zwei Beschreibungen bleiben übrig.
practice_sentences.en: Two descriptions remain (unused).
practice_sentences.hint: Like Lesen Teil 3, there are distractors

practice_sentences.de: Konzentrieren Sie sich auf die Unterschiede zwischen ähnlichen Optionen.
practice_sentences.en: Focus on the differences between similar options.
practice_sentences.hint: The one detail that's different is the key

exam_tips.tip: In the preparation time, read ALL descriptions and mark what makes each one UNIQUE (day, time, age, location).
Listen for the ONE differentiating detail, not the general topic. Two options about a 'Kochkurs' differ by when/where/for whom.
Write your answer after EACH clip. Don't try to remember multiple answers at once.
If you miss a clip, make your best guess and focus fully on the NEXT one. Don't let one missed clip ruin the rest.

visual_summary.title: Hören Teil 3 — Selective Listening

Options differ by one key detail
Mark immediately after each clip
Read descriptions BEFORE audio starts

videos.title: Hören — Overall Strategy & Common Traps

study_notes.summary: The Hören section is worth 75 points (25% of total). It consists of three Teile played on a CD in the exam room. You cannot control playback. Total listening time is about 20-25 minutes. This section rewards preparation and calm focus.
study_notes.key_concept: Hören is often the section students fear most, but with strategy it becomes predictable. Key principle: you are NOT expected to understand every word. You need to understand enough to answer the specific question being asked.
study_notes.grammar_rule: Spoken German differs from written: reductions (hab' statt habe), filler words (also, na ja, ähm), and false starts (Ich wollte... also ich meine...). Don't let these confuse you — they're normal speech.
study_notes.word_order_note: In spoken German, speakers often use verb-second order even in subordinate clauses: 'weil ich hab' keine Zeit' instead of correct 'weil ich keine Zeit habe'. Both mean the same thing.
common_mistakes: Not using preparation time to read questions/options before audio
Panicking after Teil 1 (heard once) and losing focus for Teil 2-3
Trying to take detailed notes instead of listening and marking
Forgetting to transfer answers to the Antwortbogen

vocabulary.example_de: Die Hörprüfung dauert etwa 25 Minuten.
vocabulary.example_en: The listening exam lasts about 25 minutes.

vocabulary.example_de: Sie hören jetzt die Aufnahme.
vocabulary.example_en: You will now hear the recording.

vocabulary.example_de: Sie hören den Text einmal / zweimal.
vocabulary.example_en: You hear the text once / twice.

vocabulary.example_de: In der Pause lesen Sie die nächste Aufgabe.
vocabulary.example_en: During the pause, read the next task.

vocabulary.example_de: Konzentrieren Sie sich auf die Schlüsselwörter.
vocabulary.example_en: Concentrate on the keywords.

vocabulary.example_de: Lassen Sie sich nicht ablenken.
vocabulary.example_en: Don't let yourself be distracted.

vocabulary.example_de: Füllwörter wie 'also' und 'na ja' sind normal.
vocabulary.example_en: Filler words like 'also' and 'na ja' are normal.

vocabulary.example_de: Manchmal gibt es Hintergrundgeräusche.
vocabulary.example_en: Sometimes there are background noises.

grammar_patterns.structure: also, na ja, ähm, ich meine, sozusagen, gewissermaßen
grammar_patterns.example_de: Also, ich finde, na ja, das ist schwierig, sozusagen.
grammar_patterns.example_en: Well, I think, you know, it's difficult, so to speak.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Don't be confused by fillers — skip over them mentally to catch the real content

grammar_patterns.example_de: Spoken: 'weil ich hab' keine Zeit' = Written: 'weil ich keine Zeit habe'
grammar_patterns.example_en: Both mean 'because I have no time'
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Accept both word orders when listening — focus on meaning, not grammar

grammar_patterns.structure: eigentlich, irgendwie, so ein bisschen, nicht unbedingt
grammar_patterns.example_de: Eigentlich finde ich das nicht so gut.
grammar_patterns.example_en: Actually, I don't think that's so good.
grammar_patterns.telc_relevance: Softeners signal nuanced opinions — 'eigentlich nicht' ≈ 'no'

flashcards.back: 75 points — 25% of total. Three parts: Teil 1 (1x), Teil 2 (2x), Teil 3 (1x)

flashcards.front: Which Teil do you hear twice?
flashcards.back: Teil 2 only. Teil 1 and Teil 3 are heard once.
Use preparation time to READ QUESTIONS FIRST. Never listen 'blind'.
Filler words: also, na ja, ähm. They carry no meaning — mentally skip them.
Means 'not really / actually no' — a soft negation. The answer is essentially negative.

flashcards.front: How to prepare for Hören at home?
flashcards.back: Listen to German podcasts/radio daily, even 10 min. Deutsche Welle Langsam Gesprochene Nachrichten is ideal.

flashcards.front: What if the exam room is noisy?
flashcards.back: Raise your hand and tell the examiner. You have the right to hear clearly.
Yes — after Hören ends, you get time to transfer to Antwortbogen. Don't forget!

practice_sentences.de: Nutzen Sie jede Pause, um die nächsten Fragen zu lesen.
practice_sentences.en: Use every pause to read the next questions.

practice_sentences.de: Hören Sie jeden Tag 10 Minuten deutsche Nachrichten.
practice_sentences.en: Listen to 10 minutes of German news every day.

practice_sentences.de: Lassen Sie sich von Füllwörtern nicht verwirren.
practice_sentences.en: Don't let filler words confuse you.

practice_sentences.de: Schreiben Sie wichtige Zahlen und Daten sofort auf.
practice_sentences.en: Write down important numbers and dates immediately.

practice_sentences.de: Bleiben Sie ruhig, auch wenn Sie etwas nicht verstehen.
practice_sentences.en: Stay calm even if you don't understand something.

exam_tips.tip: Daily listening practice for 2 weeks before the exam makes more difference than grammar cramming. Listen to DW Langsam Gesprochene Nachrichten.
In the exam, sit near the speaker/CD player if you can choose your seat.
If you completely miss a Teil 1 or Teil 3 clip, guess and move on. Dwelling on it ruins the next question.
Practice with real TELC mock exams at exam speed. Slowing down audio while practicing gives a false sense of security.
